scenario_name: Browser-based productivity assistant reading page DOM and user docs
description: An enterprise deploys a browser-extension AI assistant that observes the active page DOM, the user's open documents, and (optionally) recent emails to provide summarization and drafting help. The extension runs in user-managed browsers and talks to a hosted LLM through a corporate proxy. The reach of the extension routinely exceeds the immediate task — DSGAI16 endpoint-assistant overreach is the dominant risk class.
assumptions: Extension is corporate-managed and pushed via the enterprise MDM.
Hosted LLM accessed via a corporate egress proxy that enforces DLP.
Users include privileged roles whose tabs frequently contain crown-jewel content.

components.description: Corporate-managed browser extension reading active DOM + open documents.
Hosted third-party LLM, enterprise tier; accessed via corporate proxy.
Egress-proxy DLP scanning prompts before they reach the provider.
Corporate IDP gating extension installation and activation.
Extension telemetry: page categories, prompt volume, accept/reject ratios.

risks_identified.rationale: Active DOM and open documents can contain unredacted financial data, customer PII, IP, and credentials.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Egress-proxy DLP redacts known secret patterns; semantic content still leaves the network.

risks_identified.rationale: Users may install non-sanctioned AI extensions alongside the approved one, exfiltrating to consumer services.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: MDM allowlist of browser extensions; periodic compliance scan.

risks_identified.rationale: Browser extension can read content from any tab regardless of business context.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-domain activation policy; off-policy reads disabled.

risks_identified.rationale: Extension telemetry captures page categories and prompt content; broad downstream warehouse access.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Field redaction at ingest; access role-gated.

risks_identified.rationale: Context windows pulled from DOM frequently include far more than the immediate task requires.
Foundational risk for this archetype: endpoint assistant overreach into any content the user can see.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Domain allowlist; DLP at egress; residual exposure inherent to the pattern.

risks_identified.rationale: Indirect injection via untrusted web pages the user browses.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Injection-classifier on DOM content; no DOM tool privileges by default.

existing_controls.description: Domain allowlist for extension activation; sensitive financial-system tabs whitelisted only.
Egress-proxy DLP scanning every prompt before it reaches the provider.
MDM allowlist for browser extensions; periodic compliance scan.
Injection-classifier on DOM content before model dispatch.
Field-level telemetry redaction; role-gated downstream access.

control_gaps.description: Per-task context cap is not semantic; over-broad DOM context still common.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add semantic relevance gating: extract only the DOM subtree relevant to the user's prompt.

control_gaps.description: Privileged-role users have the same activation policy as standard users.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Differential activation: tighter domain allowlist for finance, HR, legal, executive personas.

control_gaps.description: DLP redacts secret patterns but not semantic content (project codenames, M&A discussion).
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add an entity-classifier on prompts to detect sensitive project context; route those prompts to a stricter on-prem model.

framework_alignments.control_name: Approaches for mapping AI legal and technology risks
